ddls:
CREATE TABLE "states" (
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each state */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Name of the state */,
  "state_code" TEXT /* State code (e.g., AP for Andhra Pradesh) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the state */,
  "area" TEXT /* Area of the state in square kilometers */,
  "capital" TEXT /* Capital city of the state */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "districts" (
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each district */,
  "district_name" TEXT /* Name of the district */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* ID of the state where the district is located */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the district */,
  "area" TEXT /* Area of the district in square kilometers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("district_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_districts_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "victims" (
  "victim_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each victim */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the victim */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the victim */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the victim */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the victim */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the victim */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("victim_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "perpetrators" (
  "perpetrator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each perpetrator */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the perpetrator */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the perpetrator */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the perpetrator */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the perpetrator */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the perpetrator */,
  "criminal_history" TEXT /* Any previous criminal history of the perpetrator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("perpetrator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crime_types" (
  "crime_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each crime type */,
  "crime_type" TEXT /* Type of the crime */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the crime type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("crime_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "police_stations" (
  "police_station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each police station */,
  "police_station_name" TEXT /* Name of the police station */,
  "district_id" INTEGER /* ID of the district where the police station is located */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the police station */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the police station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("police_station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_police_stations_district_id FOREIGN KEY ("district_id") REFERENCES districts ("district_id")
)
column_names: State code (e.g., AP for Andhra Pradesh)
Area of the state in square kilometers
ID of the state where the district is located
Area of the district in square kilometers
Any previous criminal history of the perpetrator
Unique identifier for each crime type
Unique identifier for each police station
ID of the district where the police station is located
Contact number of the police station
